let healthy = node_map\n461\t                    .get(node_id)\n462\t                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n463\t                    .unwrap_or(false);\n464\t                if healthy {\n465\t                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n466\t                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n467\t                }\n468\t            }\n469\t        }\n470\t        if has_healthy_replica {\n471\t            healthy_shards += 1;\n472\t        } else {\n473\t            degraded_shards += 1;\n474\t        }\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n478\t        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n479\t    } else {\n480\t        1.0\n481\t    };\n482\t    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n483\t    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n484\t\n485\t    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n486\t        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n487\t    }\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n491\t///\n492\t/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n493\t/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n494\t/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\n495\tfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n496\t    // ── Memory pressure ──\n497\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n498\t    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n499\t    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n500\t\n501\t    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n502\t        if max > 0 {\n503\t            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n504\t            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n505\t            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n506\t        }\n507\t    }\n508\t\n509\t    // ── CPU throttling ──\n510\t    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n511\t    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n512\t        for line in contents.lines() {\n513\t            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n514\t                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n515\t                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n516\t                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n517\t                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n518\t                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n519\t                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n520\t                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n521\t                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n522\t                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n523\t                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n524\t                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n525\t                }\n526\t            }\n527\t        }\n528\t    }\n529\t\n530\t    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n531\t    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n532\t    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n533\t    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n534\t    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n535\t    metrics.set_leader(true);\n536\t    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\t/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\n540\tfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n541\t    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n542\t        .ok()\n543\t        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n544\t}\n545\t\n546\t/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\n547\tasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n548\t    let ctrl_c = async {\n549\t        signal::ctrl_c()\n550\t            .await\n551\t            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n552\t    };\n553\t\n554\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n555\t    let terminate = async {\n556\t        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n557\t            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n558\t            .recv()\n559\t            .await;\n560\t    };\n561\t\n562\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n563\t    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n564\t\n565\t    tokio::select! {\n566\t        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n567\t        _ = terminate => {},\n568\t    }\n569\t\n570\t    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n571\t\n572\t    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n573\t    otel::shutdown_otel();\n574\t}\n575\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"cf660532-290f-4b86-9008-9bdd4561c828","uuid":"eb4bfb55-7823-48da-b0a0-691c4a43daca","timestamp":"2026-05-03T19:52:23.295Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/main.rs","content":"use EnvFilter::new(\"info\"));\n\n    let pod_id = std::env::var(\"POD_NAME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \"unknown\".to_string());\n\n    // Build subscriber - conditionally add OTel layer\n    // Note: We .with_span_list(false);\n        // Apply OTel layer to registry first, then add filter and json layer\n        registry()\n            .with(otel_layer)\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    } else {\n        let json_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .json()\n            .flatten_event(true)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .with_current_span(true)\n            .with_span_list(false);\n        registry()\n            .with(filter)\n            .with(json_layer)\n            .init();\n    }\n\n    // Set pod_id as a global default field so it appears on every log line.\n    // This is done via a separate info span that is entered once and never\n    // left — its fields propagate to tokio::spawn(async move {\n            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n        });\n\n        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n                &redis_url,\n                &key_prefix,\n                move |session_id: String| {\n                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n                    revoked_total.inc();\n                },\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .route(\"/multi-search\", post(multi_search::multi_search::<UnifiedState>)) // §13.11\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        //\n        // To achieve this order, we add layers in REVERSE (last call = outermost):\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n    metrics.set_leader(true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":575,"startLine":1,"totalLines":575}}}

= std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n            current_uid: None,\n            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements the plan §13.1 shadow-index resharding mechanics and §15 OP#3\n4\t//! empirical validation of the 2× transient load caveat.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::router::{assign_shard_in_group, shard_for_key};\n7\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\n8\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n9\tuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n10\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n11\t\n12\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n13\t// Schedule window guard\n14\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n15\t\n16\t/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n17\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n18\tpub struct TimeWindow {\n19\t    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n20\t    pub start_mins: u16,\n21\t    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n22\t    pub end_mins: u16,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\timpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n26\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n27\t        write!(\n28\t            f,\n29\t            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n30\t            self.start_mins / 60,\n31\t            self.start_mins % 60,\n32\t            self.end_mins / 60,\n33\t            self.end_mins % 60\n34\t        )\n35\t    }\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\timpl TimeWindow {\n39\t    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n40\t    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n41\t        let (start, end) = s\n42\t            .split_once('-')\n43\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n44\t        Ok(TimeWindow {\n45\t            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n46\t            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n47\t        })\n48\t    }\n49\t\n50\t    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n51\t        let (h, m) = hm\n52\t            .split_once(':')\n53\t            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n54\t        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n55\t        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n56\t        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n57\t            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n58\t        }\n59\t        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n60\t    }\n61\t\n62\t    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n63\t    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n64\t        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n65\t            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n66\t        } else {\n67\t            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n68\t            utc_minutes Online resharding: window guard, simulation model, and six-phase execution.\n//!\n//! crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Schedule window guard\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A UTC time window like `\"02:00-06:00\"`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct TimeWindow {\n    /// Start hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub start_mins: u16,\n    /// End hour+minute in minutes since midnight UTC.\n    pub end_mins: u16,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for TimeWindow {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(\n            f,\n            \"{:02}:{:02}-{:02}:{:02}\",\n            self.start_mins / 60,\n            self.start_mins % 60,\n            self.end_mins / 60,\n            self.end_mins % 60\n        )\n    }\n}\n\nimpl TimeWindow {\n    /// Parse a `\"HH:MM-HH:MM\"` string (UTC).\n    pub fn parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {\n        let (start, end) = s\n            .split_once('-')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM, got {}\", s))?;\n        Ok(TimeWindow {\n            start_mins: Self::parse_hm(start)?,\n            end_mins: Self::parse_hm(end)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn parse_hm(hm: &str) -> Result<u16, String> {\n        let (h, m) = hm\n            .split_once(':')\n            .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid minute: {}\", m))?;\n        if h >= 24 || m >= 60 {\n            return Err(format!(\"time out of range: {}\", hm));\n        }\n        Ok(h * 60 + m)\n    }\n\n    /// Does `utc_minutes` (minutes since midnight UTC) fall inside this window?\n    pub fn contains(&self, utc_minutes: u16) -> bool {\n        if self.start_mins <= self.end_mins {\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins && utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        } else {\n            // Wraps midnight, e.g. 22:00-06:00\n            utc_minutes >= self.start_mins || utc_minutes < self.end_mins\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Resharding configuration (plan §13.1 + schedule window guard).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardingConfig {\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n allowed_windows: Vec<String>,\n}\n\nfn default_backfill_concurrency() -> usize {\n    4\n}\nfn default_backfill_batch_size() -> usize {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_true() -> bool Miroir configuration — plan §4 YAML schema with §13 advanced capabilities.\n\nmod advanced;\nmod error;\nmod load;\nmod validate;\n\npub use error::ConfigError;\npub use advanced::{SearchUiConfig, CspOverridesConfig};\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n/// Top-level configuration matching plan §4 YAML schema under `miroir:`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct MiroirConfig {\n    // --- Secrets (env-var overrides) ---\n    /// Client-facing API key. Env override: `MIROIR_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub master_key: String,\n    /// Key Miroir uses on Meilisearch nodes. Env override: `MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n\n    // --- Core topology ---\n    /// Total number of logical shards.\n    pub shards: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group replicas per shard).    pub peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig,\n    pub leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig,\n    pub hpa: HpaConfig,\n}\n\n/// Convenience alias.\npub type Config = MiroirConfig;\n\nimpl Default for cross-field constraints. Returns `Ok(())` or a `ConfigError`.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {\n        validate::validate(self)\n    }\n\n    /// Layered loading: file → env overrides → CLI overrides.\n    pub fn load() -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::load()\n    }\n\n    /// Load from a specific file path with env-var overrides applied.\n    pub fn load_from(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::load_from(path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load from a YAML string (useful for testing).\n    pub fn from_yaml(yaml: &str) -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::from_yaml(yaml)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Core sub-structs (§4)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the cluster topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeConfig {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub address: String,\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\n/// Task store backend configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct TaskStoreConfig {\n    /// `sqlite` or `redis`.\n    pub backend: String,\n    /// Path to SQLite database file (sqlite backend).\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Redis URL (redis backend), e.g. `redis://host:6379`.\n    pub url: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskStoreConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            backend: /// Fail the request if any shard is unavailable.\n    Error,\n    /// Fall back to another replica group for unavailable shards.\n    Fallback,\n}\n\nimpl Default for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Partial\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Partial => write!(f, \"partial\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n            Self::Fallback => write!(f, \"fallback\"),\n        }\n Query explain API (plan §13.20).\n//!\n//! Explains how a search query will be executed without running it,\n//! showing the chosen replica group, target nodes, and any warnings.\n\nuse crate::config::MiroirConfig;\nuse crate::query_planner::QueryPlan;\nuse crate::router::shard_for_key;\nuse crate::topology::{Topology, GroupId, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Query explanation response (plan §13.20).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QueryExplanation {\n    /// The resolved index UID (after alias resolution).\n    pub resolved_uid: String,\n    /// The execution plan.\n    pub plan: ExplainPlan,\n    /// Warnings about the query or configuration.\n    pub warnings: Vec<Warning>,\n}\n\n/// Execution plan details.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ExplainPlan {\n    /// Alias resolution, if any.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub alias_resolution: Option<AliasResolution>,\n    /// Whether the query was narrowed to a subset of shards.\n    pub narrowed: bool,\n    /// Reason for narrowing, if any.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub narrowing_reason: Option<String>,\n    /// Target shard IDs (empty if narrowed=false means all shards).\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    /// The chosen replica group for this query.\n    pub chosen_group: ChosenGroup,\n    /// Target node mapping: shard_id -> node_id.\n    pub target_nodes: HashMap<String, String>,\n    /// Whether hedging is armed for this query.\n    pub hedging_armed: bool,\n    /// Hedge trigger time in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub hedge_trigger_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// Whether query coalescing is eligible.\n    pub coalescing_eligible: bool,\n    /// Whether this query is a cache candidate.\n    pub cache_candidate: bool,\n    /// Tenant affinity pinning, if any.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub tenant_affinity_pinned: Option<GroupAffinity>,\n    /// Estimated p95 latency in milliseconds.\n    pub estimated_p95_ms: f64,\n    /// Current settings version.\n    pub settings_version: u64,\n    /// Whether a settings broadcast is currently in flight.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub broadcast_pending: Option<BroadcastPending>,\n}\n\n/// Alias resolution details.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AliasResolution {\n    /// The original alias name.\n    pub from: String,\n    /// The resolved index UID.\n    pub to: String,\n    /// The alias version.\n    pub version: u64,\n}\n\n/// Chosen replica group details.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ChosenGroup {\n    /// The group ID.\n    pub id: u32,\n    /// Why this group was chosen.\n    pub reason: String,\n}\n\n/// Tenant affinity pinning.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GroupAffinity {\n    /// The tenant ID.\n    pub tenant: String,\n    /// The pinned group ID.\n    pub group: u32,\n}\n\n/// Broadcast pending details.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BroadcastPending {\n    /// The proposed settings fingerprint.\n    pub fingerprint: String,\n    /// Expected time to commit (human-readable).\n    pub commit_in: String,\n}\n\n/// Warning types.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(tag = \"type\")]\npub enum Warning {\n    /// Filter references unfilterable attribute.\n    UnfilterableAttribute {\n        attribute: String,\n        suggestion: String,\n    },\n    /// Very large offset+limit triggers per-shard over-fetch.\n    LargeOffsetLimit {\n        offset: usize,\n        limit: usize,\n        total: usize,\n        suggestion: String,\n    },\n    /// Unbounded wildcard query.\n    UnboundedWildcard {\n        query: String,\n    },\n    /// Settings drift detected across nodes.\n    SettingsDrift {\n        index: String,\n        versions: HashMap<NodeId, u64>,\n    },\n    /// Tenant affinity mismatch.\n    TenantAffinityMismatch {\n        tenant: String,\n        expected_group: u32,\n        actual_group: u32,\n    },\n    /// Shard-aware narrowing not possible.\n    NarrowingNotPossible {\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    /// Settings broadcast in flight.\n    SettingsBroadcastInFlight {\n        commit_in: String,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Explainer for queries.\npub struct Explainer {\n    config: MiroirConfig,\n}\n\nimpl Explainer {\n    /// Create a new explainer.\n    pub fn new(config: MiroirConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Explain a search query.\n    ///\n    /// Takes the same request body as `/search` but returns the plan\n    /// without executing it.\n    pub fn explain(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        query: &SearchQueryExplanation,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        settings_version: u64,\n        broadcast_pending: Option<&BroadcastPending>,\n    ) -> QueryExplanation {\n        let mut warnings = Vec::new();\n\n        // Resolve alias (if applicable)\n        let (resolved_uid, alias_resolution) = self.resolve_alias(index_uid, topology);\n\n        // Build query plan using the query planner\n        let plan_result = crate::query_planner::build_plan(\n            &resolved_uid,\n            &query.filter,\n            &query.sort,\n            topology,\n            &self.config,\n        );\n\n        let target_shards: Vec<u32> = plan_result\n            .narrowed_shards\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| (0..topology.shards).collect());\n\n        let narrowed = plan_result.narrowed_shards.is_some();\n\n        if let Some(reason) = plan_result.narrowing_reason {\n            warnings.push(Warning::NarrowingNotPossible { reason });\n        }\n\n        // Choose replica group\n        let chosen_group = self.choose_group(topology, &query.tenant_id, settings_version);\n\n        // Map shards to nodes\n        let target_nodes = self.map_shards_to_nodes(&target_shards, chosen_group.id, topology);\n\n        // Check for hedging\n        let hedging_armed = self.config.hedging.enabled;\n        let hedge_trigger_ms = if     .ok_or_else(|| format!(\"expected HH:MM, got {}\", hm))?;\n54\t        let h: u16 = h.parse().map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid hour: {}\", h))?;\n55\t        let m: u16 = m.parse().map_err(|_| }\n143\t\n144\t    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n145\t        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n146\t            Ok(w) => w,\n147\t            Err(_) => continue,\n148\t        };\n149\t        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n150\t            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n151\t                window: raw.clone(),\n152\t            };\n153\t        }\n154\t }\n160\t}\n161\t\n162\t/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\n163\tpub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n164\t    let utc_minute = f64,\n233\t\n234\t    /// Backfill duration in seconds (at configured throttle).\n235\t    pub backfill_duration_secs: f64,\n236\t    /// Total bytes written during full reshard operation.\n237\t    pub total_bytes_written: u64,\n238\t\n239\t    /// Per-node peak storage (bytes).\n240\t    pub per_node_peak_storage_bytes: u64,\n241\t    /// Per-node normal storage (bytes).\n242\t    pub per_node_normal_storage_bytes: u64,\n243\t\n244\t    /// Hash distribution stats for old shards.\n245\t    pub old_shard_cv: f64,\n246\t    /// Hash distribution stats for new shards.\n247\t    pub new_shard_cv: f64,\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t/// Run a resharding load simulation with the given parameters.\n251\t///\n252\t/// This models the six-phase resharding process from plan §13.1 using\n253\t/// the actual routing code to compute shard assignments and estimate\n254\t/// storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n255\t/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\n256\tpub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n257\t    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n258\t    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n259\t    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n260\t    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n261\t\n262\t    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n263\t    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n264\t        .map(|g| {\n265\t            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n266\t            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n267\t                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n268\t            }\n269\t            group\n270\t        })\n271\t        .collect();\n272\t\n273\t    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n274\t    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n275\t    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.old_shards as usize];\n276\t    let mut new_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; params.new_shards as usize];\n277\t\n278\t    // Track per-node storage for old and new shard assignments.\n279\t    // Each group stores the full corpus; each node in a group stores its\n280\t    // rendezvous-assigned fraction.\n281\t    let total_nodes = (rg as usize) * nodes_per_group;\n282\t    let mut node_storage_old: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n283\t    let mut node_storage_new: Vec<u64> = vec![0; total_nodes];\n284\t\n285\t    for i in 0..total_docs {\n286\t        let key = format!(\"doc-{}\", i);\n287\t        let old_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.old_shards);\n288\t        let new_shard = shard_for_key(&key, params.new_shards);\n289\t\n290\t        old_shard_counts[old_shard as usize] += 1;\n291\t        new_shard_counts[new_shard as usize] += 1;\n292\t\n293\t        // For each replica group, assign shard to RF nodes.\n294\t        for (g_idx, group) in groups.iter().enumerate() {\n295\t            let old_targets = assign_shard_in_group(old_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n296\t            let new_targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), rf);\n297\t\n298\t            for node_id in &old_targets {\n299\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n300\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n301\t                node_storage_old[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n302\t            }\n303\t            for node_id in &new_targets {\n304\t                let node_idx = g_idx * nodes_per_group\n305\t                    + group.nodes().iter().position(|n| n == node_id).unwrap_or(0);\n306\t                node_storage_new[node_idx] += params.doc_size_bytes;\n307\t            }\n308\t        }\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    // Compute distribution coefficients of variation.\n312\t    let old_cv = cv(&old_shard_counts);\n313\t    let new_cv = cv(&new_shard_counts);\n314\t\n315\t    // Normal storage: corpus replicated across RG groups.\n316\t    let normal_storage_bytes = params.corpus_size_bytes * rg as u64;\n317\t    // Peak storage: live + shadow (both fully populated).\n318\t    let peak_storage_bytes = normal_storage_bytes * 2;\n319\t\n320\t    // Per-node storage (max across all nodes).\n321\t    let per_node_normal = node_storage_old.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n322\t    let per_node_peak = per_node_normal + node_storage_new.iter().copied().max().unwrap_or(0);\n323\t\n324\t    // Write rates.\n325\t    // Normal: each incoming doc → RF × RG actual node writes.\n326\t    let normal_write_rate = params.write_rate_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n327\t    // Dual-write: each incoming doc → 2 × (RF × RG) writes (old + new assignment).\n328\t    let dual_write_rate = normal_write_rate * 2;\n329\t    // Backfill: reads all docs, writes each to new assignment → RF × RG writes/doc.\n330\t    // Plus ongoing dual-writes for new incoming docs.\n331\t    let backfill_write_rate = params.backfill_throttle_dps * rf as u64 * rg as u64;\n332\t    let peak_write_rate = dual_write_rate + backfill_write_rate;\n333\t\n334\t    let dual_write_amplification = 2.0;\n335\t    let peak_write_amplification = peak_write_rate as f64 / normal_write_rate as f64;\n336\t\n337\t    // Backfill duration: total docs / throttle rate.\n338\t    let backfill_duration_secs = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n339\t        total_docs as f64 / params.backfill_throttle_dps as f64\n340\t    } else {\n341\t        f64::INFINITY\n342\t    };\n343\t\n344\t    // Total bytes written during reshard:\n345\t    // 1. Dual-write ongoing for the full reshard duration.\n346\t    // 2. Backfill writes of entire corpus.\n347\t    // Approximate: backfill_duration × dual_write_rate + corpus × RF × RG.\n348\t    let total_reshard_write_bytes = if params.backfill_throttle_dps > 0 {\n349\t        let dual_write_bytes =\n350\t            backfill_duration_secs * dual_write_rate as f64 * params.doc_size_bytes as f64;\n351\t        let backfill_bytes = total_docs * rf as u64 * rg as u64 * params.doc_size_bytes;\n352\t        (dual_write_bytes as u64) + backfill_bytes\n353\t    } else {\n354\t        0\n355\t    };\n356\t\n357\t    let storage_amplification = peak_storage_bytes as f64 / normal_storage_bytes as f64;\n358\t\n359\t    SimResult {\n360\t        label: format!(\n361\t            \"{}KB/{}GB/RG{}/RF{}\",\n362\t            params.doc_size_bytes / 1024,\n363\t            params.corpus_size_bytes / (1024 * 1024 * 1024),\n364\t            rg,\n365\t            rf\n366\t        ),\n367\t        doc_size_bytes: params.doc_size_bytes,\n368\t        corpus_size_bytes: params.corpus_size_bytes,\n369\t        total_docs,\n370\t        replica_groups: rg,\n371\t        replication_factor: rf,\n372\t        old_shards: params.old_shards,\n373\t        new_shards: params.new_shards,\n374\t        nodes_per_group,\n375\t        write_rate_dps: params.write_rate_dps,\n376\t\n377\t        normal_storage_bytes,\n378\t        peak_storage_bytes,\n379\t        storage_amplification,\n380\t\n381\t        normal_write_rate,\n382\t        dual_write_rate,\n383\t        peak_write_rate,\n384\t        dual_write_amplification,\n385\t        peak_write_amplification,\n386\t\n387\t        backfill_duration_secs,\n388\t        total_bytes_written: total_reshard_write_bytes,\n389\t\n390\t        per_node_peak_storage_bytes: per_node_peak,\n391\t        per_node_normal_storage_bytes: per_node_normal,\n392\t\n393\t        old_shard_cv: old_cv,\n394\t        new_shard_cv: new_cv,\n395\t    }\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\t/// Coefficient of variation for a distribution.\n399\tfn cv(values: &[u64]) -> f64 {\n400\t    if values.is_empty() {\n401\t        return 0.0;\n402\t    }\n403\t    let n = values.len() as f64;\n404\t    let mean = values.iter().sum::<u64>() as f64 / n;\n405\t    if mean == 0.0 {\n406\t        return 0.0;\n407\t    }\n408\t    let variance = values\n409\t        .iter()\n410\t        .map(|v| (*v as f64 - mean).powi(2))\n411\t        .sum::<f64>()\n412\t        / n;\n413\t    variance.sqrt() / mean\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n417\t// Tests\n418\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n419\t\n420\t#[cfg(test)]\n421\tmod tests {\n422\t    use super::*;\n423\t\n424\t    // ---- TimeWindow parsing and containment ----\n425\t\n426\t    #[test]\n427\t    fn time_window_parse_simple() {\n428\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n429\t        assert_eq!(w.start_mins, 120);\n430\t        assert_eq!(w.end_mins, 360);\n431\t    }\n432\t\n433\t    #[test]\n434\t    fn time_window_parse_wrap_midnight() {\n435\t        let w = 01:40\n445\t        assert!(!w.contains(400)); // 06:40\n446\t    }\n447\t\n448\t    #[test]\n449\t    fn time_window_contains_wrap() {\n450\t        let w =    }\n455\t\n456\t    #[test]\n457\t    fn time_window_boundary_start() {\n458\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00-06:00\").unwrap();\n459\t        assert!(w.contains(120)); // exactly assert!(!w.contains(360)); // exactly 06:00 is excluded\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    #[test]\n469\t    fn time_window_invalid_format() {\n470\t }\n474\t\n475\t    // ---- Window guard ----\n476\t\n477\t    #[test]\n478\t    fn window_guard_no_restriction() {\n479\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n480\t        assert_eq!(check_window(0, &config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction);\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    #[test]\n484\t    fn window_guard_allowed() {\n485\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n486\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-06:00\".into()],\n487\t            ..Default::default()\n488\t        };\n489\t        let result = check_window(180, &config); // 03:00\n490\t        assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Allowed { .. }));\n491\t    }\n492\t\n493\t    #[test]\n494\t    fn window_guard_denied() {\n495\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n496\t assert!(matches!(result, WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }));\n501\t    }\n502\t\n503\t    #[test]\n504\t    fn window_guard_multiple_windows() {\n505\t        let config = ReshardingConfig {\n506\t            allowed_windows: vec![\"02:00-04:00\".into(), \"22:00-23:30\".into()],\n507\t            ..Default::default()\n508\t        };\n509\t        // In first window.\n510\t assert!(matches!(\n521\t            check_window(720, &config),\n522\t            WindowGuardResult::Denied { .. }\n523\t        ));\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    // ---- Simulation ----\n527\t\n528\t    #[test]\n529\t    fn simulation_storage_always_2x() {\n530\t        // Regardless of parameters, peak storage should be exactly 2× normal.\n531\t        let params = SimParams {\n532\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n533\t            corpus_size_bytes: 10 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024, // 10 GB\n534\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n535\t            replica_groups: 2,\n536\t            replication_factor: 1,\n537\t            old_shards: 64,\n538\t            new_shards: 128,\n539\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n540\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n541\t        };\n542\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n543\t        assert!(\n544\t            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n545\t            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n546\t            result.storage_amplification\n547\t        );\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    #[test]\n551\t    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n552\t        let params = SimParams {\n553\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n554\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n555\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n556\t            replica_groups: 2,\n557\t            replication_factor: 1,\n558\t            old_shards: 16,\n559\t            new_shards: 32,\n560\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n561\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n562\t        };\n563\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n564\t        assert!(\n565\t            (result.dual_write_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n566\t            \"expected 2.0, got {}\",\n567\t            result.dual_write_amplification\n568\t        );\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    #[test]\n572\t    fn simulation_low_cv_with_many_docs() {\n573\t        // With enough docs, hash distribution CV should be very low (< 5%).\n574\t        let params = SimParams {\n575\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n576\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n577\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n578\t            replica_groups: 1,\n579\t            replication_factor: 1,\n580\t            old_shards: 16,\n581\t            new_shards: 64,\n582\t            nodes_per_group: 4,\n583\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 1000,\n584\t        };\n585\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n586\t        assert!(\n587\t            result.old_shard_cv < 0.05,\n588\t            \"old shard CV too high: {}\",\n589\t            result.old_shard_cv\n590\t        );\n591\t        assert!(\n592\t            result.new_shard_cv < 0.05,\n593\t            \"new shard CV too high: {}\",\n594\t            result.new_shard_cv\n595\t        );\n596\t    }\n597\t\n598\t    #[test]\n599\t    fn time_window_display_roundtrip() {\n600\t        let w = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:30-06:45\").unwrap();\n601\t        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", w), \"02:30-06:45\");\n602\t    }\n603\t\n604\t    #[test]\n605\t    fn time_window_parse_missing_dash() {\n606\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:00\").unwrap_err();\n607\t        assert!(err.contains(\"expected HH:MM-HH:MM\"));\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    #[test]\n611\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_hm() {\n612\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n613\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n614\t    }\n615\t\n616\t    #[test]\n617\t    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n618\t        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n619\t        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n620\t    }\n621\t\n622\t    #[test]\n623\t    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n624\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n625\t        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n626\t    }\n627\t\n628\t    #[test]\n629\t    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n630\t        let params = SimParams {\n631\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n632\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n633\t            write_rate_dps: 100,\n634\t            replica_groups: 1,\n635\t            replication_factor: 1,\n636\t            old_shards: 4,\n637\t            new_shards: 8,\n638\t            nodes_per_group: 2,\n639\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n640\t        };\n641\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n642\t        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n643\t        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n644\t    }\n645\t\n646\t    #[test]\n647\t    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n648\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n649\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n650\t    }\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n654\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n655\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n656\t\n657\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n658\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n659\t#[repr(u8)]\n660\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n661\t    /// No active resharding.\n662\t    Idle = 0,\n663\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n664\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n665\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n666\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n667\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n668\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n669\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n670\t    Verifying = 4,\n671\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n672\t    Swapped = 5,\n673\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n674\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n675\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n676\t    Complete = 7,\n677\t    /// Resharding failed.\n678\t    Failed = 8,\n679\t}\n680\t\n681\timpl ReshardPhase {\n682\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n683\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n684\t        match self {\n685\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n686\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n687\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n688\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n689\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n690\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n691\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n692\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n693\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n694\t        }\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n698\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n699\t        match v {\n700\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n701\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n702\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n703\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n704\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n705\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n706\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n707\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n708\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n709\t            _ => None,\n710\t        }\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n715\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n716\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n717\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n718\t    pub id: String,\n719\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n720\t    pub index_uid: String,\n721\t    /// Old shard count.\n722\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n723\t    /// New shard count.\n724\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n725\t    /// Current phase.\n726\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n727\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n728\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n729\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n730\t    pub created_at: u64,\n731\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n732\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n733\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n734\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n735\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n736\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n737\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n738\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n739\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n740\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n741\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n742\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n746\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n747\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n748\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n749\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n750\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n751\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n752\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n753\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n754\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n755\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n756\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n757\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n758\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n759\t    pub passed: bool,\n760\t}\n761\t\n762\timpl ReshardOperation {\n763\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n764\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n765\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n766\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n767\t        let now = millis_now();\n768\t        Self {\n769\t            id,\n770\t            index_uid,\n771\t            old_shards,\n772\t            target_shards,\n773\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n774\t            phase_started_at: now,\n775\t            created_at: now,\n776\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n777\t            total_documents: 0,\n778\t            last_error: None,\n779\t            shadow_index,\n780\t            verification_results: None,\n781\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n782\t        }\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n786\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n787\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n788\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n789\t    }\n790\t\n791\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n792\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n793\t        self.last_error = Some(error);\n794\t        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    /// Update backfill progress.\n798\t    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n799\t        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n800\t        self.total_documents = total;\n801\t    }\n802\t\n803\t    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n804\t    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n805\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n806\t            return 0.0;\n807\t        }\n808\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n812\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n813\t        matches!(\n814\t            self.phase,\n815\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n816\t        )\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n820\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n821\t        &self.shadow_index\n822\t    }\n823\t}\n824\t\n825\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n826\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n827\t    SystemTime::now()\n828\t fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n851\t        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n852\t            return Err(format!(\n853\t                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n854\t                op.index_uid, existing_id\n855\t            ));\n856\t        }\n857\t   }\n878\t\n879\t    /// Update an operation.\n880\t    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n881\t        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n882\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n883\t        }\n884\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n885\t        Ok(())\n886\t    }\n887\t\n888\t    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n889\t    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n890\t        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n891\t        if !op.is_terminal() {\n892\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n893\t        }\n894\t        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n895\t        Ok(())\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    /// List all operations.\n899\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n900\t        let now = millis_now();\n906\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n907\t        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n908\t            if op.is_terminal() && }\n913\t        for id in to_remove {\n914\t            self.operations.remove(&id);\n915\t        }\n916\t    }\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t#[cfg(test)]\n920\tmod tests_reshard_execution {\n921\t    use
